meta.note: Curated, sanitized snapshot of the unsigned platform engineering roadmap. Edit this file to evolve the public roadmap; the page renders from it. No internal identifiers, hostnames, or credentials — public-site data honesty applies.

items.title: Self-hosted CI/CD with enforced review
items.blurb: Code review and merge move onto self-hosted Forgejo: required approvals, signed commits, and CI as real merge-blocking checks. GitHub stays a downstream mirror so releases and issue-linking keep working.

items.title: One keyless secret store
items.blurb: Every credential consolidates onto OpenBao behind OIDC-brokered auth. The goal is blunt: no secret ever copied by hand, no long-lived tokens sitting on disk.

items.title: Single sign-on across the platform
items.blurb: Keycloak OIDC with Google Workspace brokering — one login for the registry, dashboards, GitOps, and internal tools, with group-based access.

items.title: Observability baseline
items.blurb: Prometheus, Grafana, Loki and tracing wired across the cluster; service health is visible end to end rather than inferred.

items.title: GPU inference serving
items.blurb: Triton-based model serving with scale-to-zero and fractional-GPU scheduling, so idle models cost nothing and hot ones burst.

items.title: GitOps that tells the truth
items.blurb: Per-app deployment definitions and sync health gated on real apply state — so a green 'Healthy' can never hide a failed change underneath.

items.title: Self-serve engineer onboarding
items.blurb: One flow from zero to operating the platform: access, identity, and a working kubeconfig, without a human in the loop for each step.

items.title: Baremetal capacity
items.blurb: Extend beyond managed Kubernetes onto self-run baremetal for denser, cheaper GPU — managed and baremetal under one control plane.

items.title: Sandboxed multi-tenant compute
items.blurb: Kernel-isolated workloads and per-tenant namespaces so shared execution is safe by construction, not by trust.

items.title: Cost transparency
items.blurb: Real per-service cost surfaced inside the platform. Transparency about spend is identity here, not a feature.
